Container.Name: SeerrDiscordBot
Container.Overview: A Discord bot that lets users request movies and TV shows via slash commands, routed through your Overseerr or Jellyseerr instance. Supports separate libraries (Movies, Kids Movies, TV Shows, Kids TV Shows), Discord-to-Seerr user linking, and availability notifications.
Container.Requires: A Discord Bot Token (from https://discord.com/developers/applications) and a running Overseerr or Jellyseerr instance.

Config.Name: Discord Bot Token
Config.Description: Your Discord bot token. Get it from https://discord.com/developers/applications → Your App → Bot → Reset Token.

Config.Name: Discord Client ID
Config.Description: Your Discord application's Client ID (also called Application ID). Found at https://discord.com/developers/applications → Your App → General Information.

Config.Name: Discord Server ID
Config.Description: Your Discord server ID. Enable Developer Mode in Discord (Settings → Advanced), then right-click your server and select Copy Server ID.

Config.Name: Requests Channel ID
Config.Description: The Discord channel ID where new requests will be posted. Right-click the channel and select Copy Channel ID.

Config.Name: Notify Channel ID
Config.Description: The Discord channel ID where availability notifications will be sent (e.g. when a requested movie is ready). Can be the same as Requests Channel. Right-click the channel and select Copy Channel ID.

Config.Name: Seerr URL
Config.Description: The URL of your Overseerr or Jellyseerr instance, including port. Example: http://192.168.1.100:5055

Config.Name: Seerr API Key
Config.Description: Your Overseerr or Jellyseerr API key. Found in Jellyseerr/Overseerr → Settings → General → API Key.

Config.Name: Allowed Role ID
Config.Description: Optional. If set, only Discord users with this role can use /request commands. Leave blank to allow everyone. Right-click a role in Server Settings → Roles to copy its ID.

Config.Name: Admin Role ID
Config.Description: Optional. If set, only Discord users with this role can use /linklist. Leave blank to allow everyone. Right-click a role in Server Settings → Roles to copy its ID.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Path to store persistent data (user links and request tracking). This data survives container restarts and updates.
